title: Level 8.88
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=April 19, 2026|Forgettable page, with nothing special. Some parts are a bit unnecessary, "filler" as well. How so? It details the exact type a lightbulb is, and theres a whole paragraph about lightbulbs. There is also a narrative which adds almost nothing, other than warning us that the entity we are about to read is scary and dangerous. This needs a lot more time in the oven}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 8.88: "Mulberry-tinted Cave-teeth"}}
{{Blurbackground|file=Purplecave.jpg|opacity=0.5}} {{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}}{{Image Banner|caption=Never knew purple could be this... formidable.|image=Purplecave.jpg}}{{Class 3}} '''Level 8.88''' is a sublevel of Level 8, which is the 9th level of the [[Backrooms Wiki|backrooms]]. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Purplecave.jpg|caption=The first known image of the lilac caves of Level 8.88}} '''Level 8.88''' is an expansive subterranean complex consisting of hundreds of caverns. The structure of '''Level 8.88''' can make it difficult to traverse, which has resulted in multiple casualties throughout the level's history. The layout of '''Level 8.88''' is very expansive and complex, sprawling with stalagmites, stalactites and other speleothems. Occasionally, there might be more open caves, with their speleothems having collapsed due to an entity that roams this level. Some caves can also have small pockets of water and inaccessible openings that lead to an unexplored surface. A well-known characteristic of '''Level 8.88''' is the purple stone that lines the caverns of it. This stone can vary wildly in composition, with some variants being more rough in texture than others which are more smooth.Though not completely. The hardness of this rock is about the same as tuffTuff ranges from a 3 to 6 on the Mohs scale., though it is unknown which kind of rock type it is. The tint of the rock can also change slightly in hue which varies from a reddish-purple to a bluish-purple, and in rare cases not even purple at all.This is rare to come across, but can be emulated by pouring [[Object 1|almond water]] on the stones. Occasionally, lightbulbs can be seen infused with '''Level 8.88's''' mauve rock. These lightbulbs emit green light and seem to have placed in strategic locations around the level, like in tunnels with a lack of external light. These lightbulbs seem to be of an E27 kind, though do not have the same properties as those found in the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. These lightbulbs are mysteriously hard to break, and have a special property of regeneration after they are broken. Rarely in this level, taller and more artificial caves can be found, similar to like that of a hive. It has been rumoured that an intelligent bug-like species used to roam this level, with these caves being their last remnants. No more is known about this suspected species, though. == Addendum ==
Expedition Log: "The Mole"
[[File:The MEG's Logo (Thanks to Egglord1).png|right|215x215px|link=]] '''Notice:'''
Data retrieval successful! '''Fetching data...'''
''15 January, 2015 - Monday, 8:11 P.M.''
"Dear backrooms wanderers and explorers: I have encountered an entity roaming Level 8.88, and it is one of the most grotesque things I have ever seen. I am just going to name it as "the mole". Look up an image of the Star-nosed mole. Disgusting, right? Now imagine that, but 11 feet tall and on the lookout for unsuspecting wanderers like us. This is what I encountered yesterday in this level. I was trying to map out the levels caves and caverns and uncover the mystery of the green lightbulbs, when I heard several stomps eastward of my position. At first, I thought a cave was falling apart. "Great," I thought to myself. I had spent so long trying to map it out. But, it turns out, the problem was much worse. A loud, ear-piercing scream echoed across the room, wobbling and disconnecting the stalactites from the room. Then I saw the beast. Instantly, it had its eyes looking straight in my direction. The mole was crawling towards me at a shockingly fast pace. I ran, I kept running. All of a sudden, I met a dead end and I hit my face on the wall. But, just when it seemed as if it was over, the mole did one final ear-shattering scream. Because of this scream, a group of stalactites fell and pierced the monster's skin, causing it to die. So remember, if you hear some unusual sounds in Level 8.88, run. And quickly too."[sic]
== Entities == The only known entities in '''Level 8.88''' are [[Entity 3|smilers]] and '''chonk-moles'''. {{Blurimage|file=Chonkmole.jpg|caption=A Chonk-mole in pursuit of a wanderer.}} === Chonk-moles === '''Chonk-moles''' (scientific name Talpidaeon bestialiminalis) are hostile entities that reside on '''Level 8.88'''. They are roughly 11 meters in height and are hostile to wanderers, even when not provoked. '''Chonk-moles''' are similar to the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] mole known as star-nosed moles. These '''Chonk-moles''' often use echolocation due to their sight not being reliable enough to hunt in '''Level 8.88''''s purple caverns. You can identify when a '''Chonk-mole''' is in your vicinity when you can hear their loud footsteps and their ear-piercing echolocation, it is advised to leave the area. '''Chonk-moles''' can crawl, run bipedally and even burrow in rock, which makes them much stronger than usual moles. This is due to their powerful claws, which can easily inflict fatal injuries to those who encounter them. With a weapon as powerful as a shotgun, '''Chonk-moles''' can be killed, which is unfortunate that most wanderers of '''Level 8.88''' do not have such weapons on hand. If the claws of a '''Chonk-mole''' are damaged, they can use stalagmites to attack and burrow instead. This practice has allowed them to somewhat use tools, which puts this species in the early stone age.By "Stone age" it is like that of a Capuchin monkey. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the nature and state of '''Level 8.88''', no colonies or outposts have been formed. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === *Finding a purple void in [[Level 6]] will lead here. *Finding a lightbulb in the caverns of [[Level 2.1]] will send you here. *Licking a purple rock in [[Level 8]] will lead you here. === Exits === *Jumping in a large pit of water will send you to [[Level 8]]. *Hugging a particularly icy speleothem will lead you to [[Level 202.1]]. *Finding a particularly spikey crystal will teleport you to [[Level 8.1]]. {{Footnotes}}{{Redirect|Prev=8.5|Next=9}} ---- {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:EnphosianUnphosian|EnphosianUnphosian]]
